meta.title: PsychSim neural-substrate SEED dataset
meta.purpose: Fix the schema for the redone comprehensive assembly and populate the honesty-critical core (innate wiring, inputs, endowment, plasticity rules, gaps).
principles: ASSEMBLE comprehensively from every reputable source; do not cherry-pick; catalogue gaps.
Structure exists in full; ACTIVATION is a gated, progressive subset (two switches: developmental_online = biology, calibration_active = tuning).
Default weights are the NEWBORN's initial conditions, modified by experience; adult patterns are EMERGENT validation targets, not inputs.

Plasticity rules are LOCAL and MEANING-BLIND: they see pre/post activity, current weight, an activity-derived modulator, and an age coefficient — never that a circuit is 'fear' or a state is 'threat'.
Innate structure is admitted ONLY as the primary-reinforcer wiring, as cited birth-present anatomy, never as an encoded verdict.
Panksepp's systems and any documented co-activation pattern are READ-OUTS / validation targets, never primitives the substrate is built from.
meta.honesty_wall: A rule may update a weight using local activity-derived quantities only. 'Co-active connections strengthen' is allowed (mechanism). 'Threat strengthens avoidance' is forbidden (outcome). The primary-reinforcer links are the single unavoidable seed of innate value and are logged as anatomy with citations.
meta.scaffold_note: Every invented dynamic (time constants, eligibility/plasticity/homeostatic timescales, structural-plasticity thresholds) is marked status:scaffold or noted SCAFFOLD, to be replaced by measured values.

assembly_status.grain: nucleus level (amygdala subnuclei kept separate: LA/BA/CeA/ITC)
assembly_status.how_value_enters: ONLY via the 3 cited innate links: IN-SOMATO:nociception->LA/CeA (PR-NOCICEPTION), SC-Pv->CeA/PAG (PB-LOOMING), StN->CeA (PB-STARTLE). Every other weight is either hardwired-backbone anatomy or a near-zero associative site grown by experience.
assembly_status.newborn_weights: qualitative (low / low-moderate / moderate / moderate-strong) because the literature gives ADULT patterns, not birth strengths; only the innate links are confidently non-zero at birth.
assembly_status.emergent_signature_expected: at birth the system can DETECT+EXPRESS threat (innate cues + CeA->PAG/HYPdm backbone) but cannot REGULATE it (vmPFC extinction, HPCv context online only from ~3y / ~14y). With early associative sites most plastic and NA-gated, early aversive experience should write strongly and resist extinction until regulation matures -- 'sticky early fear, late control' as a consequence of timing, not an authored rule.
assembly_status.schema_change: added optional 'function' field to circuit records (+ schemas.circuit); ALL prior fields and keys preserved.
assembly_status.next_system: episodic memory (hippocampal, beyond its contextual role) or motor output (closing the basal-ganglia/cerebellar loops); then the two-agent relationship matrix

reward_approach_notes.how_value_enters: ONLY via 6 cited appetitive links: IN-GUST:sweet->NAc-shell/VTA (PR-SWEET), IN-GUST:umami->NAc-shell (PR-UMAMI), IN-SOMATO:affective_touch->NAc-shell (PR-CONTACT), IN-INTERO:thermal_warmth->NAc-shell (PR-THERMAL), IN-INTERO:nutritive_state->LH (PR-HOMEOSTATIC).
reward_approach_notes.gate_mirrors_threat: VTA-dopamine gates approach plasticity exactly as LC-noradrenaline gates threat plasticity (same three-factor rule R5); no new machinery.
reward_approach_notes.negative_arm_is_structural: LHb->RMTg->VTA suppresses dopamine on worse-than-expected outcomes, so disappointment/reward-omission is produced by the wiring, not by a 'disappointment rule'.
reward_approach_notes.shared_substrate: reuses threat-system circuits: BA and HPCv supply appetitive cue-value and contextual-reward inputs to accumbens; mesocortical DA reaches the same vmPFC node used for extinction. Amygdala/hippocampus/vmPFC are bivalent, not duplicated.
reward_approach_notes.emergent_signature_expected: dopamine schedule peaks in adolescence while OFC/vmPFC valuation matures late -> strong reward learning with weak top-down valuation in adolescence (heightened reward/sensation-seeking): the reward-side counterpart of threat's 'sticky early fear, late control'. With approach and avoid now coexisting, approach/avoid conflict becomes an emergent balance.

sensory_input_notes.mostly_value_neutral: Sensory circuits are processing hierarchies carrying NO innate value; the schema handles pure relays cleanly. Value enters ONLY at cited links.
sensory_input_notes.innate_links_placed_here: PB-FACE and PB-BIOMOTION (subcortical orienting biases; FFA specialisation is experience-built, NOT innate), PB-VOICE (partly prenatal-learned), the sensory limb of PB-STARTLE (COCH->StN), and the aversive taste reinforcers PR-BITTER/PR-SOUR->CeA (completing the taste set; sweet/umami->reward were placed with reward).
sensory_input_notes.discriminative_vs_affective_pain: nociception->VPL->S1 is DISCRIMINATIVE (where/how much) and carries NO value; the AFFECTIVE value link nociception->amygdala lives in the threat system. Keeping these separate is the honesty discipline in a sensory case.
sensory_input_notes.resolves_placeholders: the generic sensory_thalamus/sensory_cortex sources used in threat/reward are now resolved into concrete modality-specific pathways (e.g. V-ventral->LA, A1-belt->LA, V-ventral->OFC); the earlier generic connections are left in place, not rewritten.
sensory_input_notes.developmental_note: experience-expectant critical periods (vision/ocular-dominance to ~7-8y; native-phoneme tuning in the first year) mean the hierarchies are shaped by early input -- deprivation yields lasting deficits. The innate subcortical detectors (looming/face/biomotion/voice) bias what the developing cortex learns from, so cortical specialisation (e.g. FFA) EMERGES from the innate orienting bias plus experience -- not authored.

executive_notes.zero_innate_value: The executive suite adds NO innate-reinforcer links -- correct and honest: it is the least innate, most experience-dependent, LATEST-maturing system (frontopolar cortex online ~12y, maturing into the 20s-30s).
executive_notes.its_job_is_regulation: Its connections are (a) WM maintenance/gating (dlPFC<->MDthal, dlPFC->Caud-assoc, fronto-parietal dlPFC<->S2-PPC), (b) inhibition/stopping (vlPFC/preSMA->STN), (c) TOP-DOWN REGULATION of the affective systems already built: dlPFC->vmPFC and vlPFC->LA down-regulate threat; dlPFC->NAc-core and OFC->dlPFC control approach/impulse; (d) top-down attention (FEF->SC-Pv/V-dorsal).
executive_notes.neuromodulatory_tuning: the ascending modulators now reach cortex: VTA-DA (D1 inverted-U), LC-NA (alpha2A; stress impairs), BF-ACh (attentional gain) tune dlPFC -- and BF-ACh also tunes sensory cortex, so the same modulators gate learning across systems.
executive_notes.capstone_signature_expected: with affective/sensory systems online and plastic EARLY but executive control online-but-weak early and maturing LAST, top-down regulation of threat (reappraisal/extinction) and reward (impulse control/stopping) is STRUCTURALLY weak in childhood/adolescence and strengthens into adulthood. This makes the earlier signatures coherent -- 'sticky early fear' and 'adolescent reward-seeking' are consequences of this maturational asymmetry, now fully represented. Whether self-regulation actually emerges (and how chaotically early on) is for the running model; it is NOT authored.
executive_notes.cross_system_integration: the executive suite binds the systems -- it uses the sensory parietal node (S2-PPC) for WM, takes value from OFC and context from HPCv, regulates amygdala and accumbens, and is tuned by the same VTA/LC/BF modulators that gate affective and sensory learning. The four systems now compose rather than sit side by side.

affiliation_social_notes.honesty_posture: STRONGEST caveat of any system so far: oxytocin/affiliation is well-evidenced in animals (vole pair-bonding) but WEAK and inconsistent in humans (intranasal-OT effects largely fail to replicate). Every affiliation circuit/connection is confidence=Em, evidence_base=animal_dominant, with the caveat in-line. Treat conclusions leaning on this system with the most caution.
affiliation_social_notes.distributed_not_siloed: Affiliation is a modulatory/distributed layer, not a box: it adds 6 nuclei (PVN-OT, MPOA, MeA, SEPT, PAG-PANIC, VMH) but works mostly through NEW CONNECTIONS onto existing nodes -- OT broadcasts to NAc (social reward), CeA (threat-damping), BNST, septum, MPOA; CARE drives VTA; separation-distress runs through a distinct PAG column; social recognition reuses olfactory (PIR) and visual (V-ventral) inputs.
affiliation_social_notes.oxytocin_is_a_5th_modulator: OT now appears as a gating_neuromodulator alongside DA/NA/ACh -- gating plasticity for SOCIAL stimuli (three-factor rule R5). Like the others it is a circuit output (PVN-OT), not a value we set.
affiliation_social_notes.one_innate_seed_only: Only ONE innate link is added -- contact-loss -> separation-distress (the infant cry is unlearned/cross-species). The POSITIVE side (contact is rewarding) was already placed via PR-CONTACT->NAc-shell in the reward system. CARE and bonding are otherwise hormone-gated and experience-built, not birth-value.
affiliation_social_notes.prerequisite_for_relationship_matrix: This is the substrate the future two-agent relationship matrix (trust/affection/love-hate) will grow from: bonding (OT/NAc), care (MPOA/VTA), separation-distress (PAG-PANIC), and social recognition (MeA) are the per-agent machinery whose dyadic history will accrue into named relationship states -- as EMERGENT read-outs, never allocated.
affiliation_social_notes.under_specified: PLAY (Panksepp) is only gestured at (PAG/thalamus + opioid/endocannabinoid modulation) and is flagged in the catalogue as mostly-animal/hypothesised in humans; a dedicated PLAY circuit is deferred. Human social-chemosignalling (vomeronasal) is vestigial and treated via main olfaction.

interoception_autonomic_notes.closes_the_body_brain_loop: This system is the felt-body substrate. Ascending limb: IN-INTERO/spinothalamic -> NTS -> parabrachial -> interoceptive thalamus (VMpo) -> posterior insula (primary) -> mid -> anterior insula ('material me', subjective feeling, emotional awareness). Descending limb: central autonomic network (amygdala/hypothalamus/mPFC/ACC) -> RVLM (sympathetic) and nucleus ambiguus/DMV (parasympathetic) -> body; the anterior insula translates interoceptive states into autonomic responses, closing the corticofugal loop.
interoception_autonomic_notes.where_emotion_is_felt: aIns -> dACC/CeA/OFC is the point at which bodily state becomes felt affect (Craig: limbic-sensory insula -> limbic-motor cingulate). This is the loop the whole model turned on, and the substrate the future relationship matrix will draw on (dyadic interoceptive/autonomic responses).
interoception_autonomic_notes.honesty: Ascending afferent + autonomic efferent anatomy and the insular hierarchy are E (human+animal). The DESCENDING predictive/allostatic framing (anterior insula issues interoceptive predictions) is Em (theoretically influential -- Barrett/Seth -- but direct causal human evidence limited). POLYVAGAL theory is NOT used (core premises widely regarded as refuted); standard two-branch autonomic organisation is used.
interoception_autonomic_notes.one_innate_alarm: One innate link: CO2/acidosis -> amygdala (ASIC1a chemosensation) -> unconditioned fear/panic + sympathetic. This is a documented INTERIOR threat cue -- the interoceptive counterpart of exterior cues like looming -- and it references the catalogue's air-hunger term (PR-HOMEOSTATIC). The positive homeostatic values (satiety, warmth) were placed earlier via PR-HOMEOSTATIC/PR-THERMAL.
interoception_autonomic_notes.loop_closure: Efferent output changes organ state, which re-enters via IN-INTERO -> NTS, so the interoceptive-autonomic loop is closed structurally. The HPA arm closes via cortisol -> glucocorticoid feedback to hippocampus/PFC/PVN.
interoception_autonomic_notes.gaps: Medullary/RTN respiratory chemoreceptors, the enteric nervous system, baroreflex interneurons (CVLM), and organ-level state are compressed or deferred; glucocorticoid effects on the amygdala are complex (not simply inhibitory) and are left qualitative.

meta.v8_addition_note: v8 adds the cognitive/mentalizing (theory-of-mind) social-cognition network -- rSMG-TPJ, pSTS, PCun-PCC, ATL-TP and their connections (see MASTER Part 3 S1). This completes the COGNITIVE-empathy side (the affective side -- aIns/dACC/OFC/amygdala -- was already present), so the CU/psychopathy lesion-throttle sweep can DISSOCIATE cognitive from affective empathy. Grounded in human meta-analyses; all dynamics (weights/tau/setpoint) are SCAFFOLD; new domain tag 'social_cognition'.
meta.v9_addition_note: v9 (Part 6 step 3e / Part 8 v9 pass): (a) NEW circuit VMHvl (hypothalamic attack area) + a provocation->attack pathway [IN-INTERO:provocation->VMHvl 'strong'; VMHvl->PAG 'strong'; VMHvl->HYPdm 'moderate-strong'] closing the OBS-3 connectome gap -- provocation could previously reach attack effectors only via the GABAergic CeA, which INHIBITS them, so more provocation deepened attack suppression. The CeA->PAG/HYPdm inhibition is UNTOUCHED (fear stays the baseline threat response); weights are SCAFFOLD, set by physiological ordering, not tuned to a target. (b) S1.4 social innate-wiring catalogue entries (SR-SEPARATION/PROXIMITY/REJECTION) documenting social primary perturbations the functional layer already wires (documentary; no dynamics change). Circuits 77->78, connections 176->179.

circuit.name: label
circuit.activation_bounds: [min,max] hard bounds for the leaky integrator
circuit.homeostatic_setpoint: target mean activity the circuit scales toward
circuit.developmental_online_age: age (years) the circuit comes online (BIOLOGY gate)
circuit.calibration_active_default: bool: is it in the live set by default (TUNING mask)
circuit.function: one-line functional role of the circuit

connection.default_weight: NEWBORN initial synaptic strength (from lit where it exists; else flagged assumption)
connection.gating_neuromodulator: DA|ACh|NA|5HT|none  (which modulator gates plasticity here; itself a circuit output)

innate_wiring_entry.mechanism_type: innate_connection (birth-present fixed wiring) | learning_bias (modifies plasticity params, NOT a weight)
innate_wiring_entry.caveat: the honest nuance / what it is NOT

physical_endowment.biases: what downstream effect it has (esp. how the world responds to the agent)

plasticity_rules.name: Rate-level (not spike-timing)
plasticity_rules.does: we use the rate-level BCM equivalent of STDP
plasticity_rules.constraint: MODELLING CHOICE: STDP's pre-before-post rule cannot be applied literally at this level; flagged, not hidden

plasticity_rules.name: BCM sliding threshold (workhorse)
plasticity_rules.form: dw prop a_pre*a_post*(a_post - theta); theta tracks recent avg a_post
plasticity_rules.does: gives potentiation above theta, depression below, competition, and self-stabilisation in one rule
plasticity_rules.constraint: theta is computed from the circuit's OWN activity — nothing external/meaningful

plasticity_rules.name: Homeostatic synaptic scaling
plasticity_rules.form: slow multiplicative scaling of incoming weights toward a set-point
plasticity_rules.constraint: must act fast enough not to be outrun by Hebbian growth (the 'temporal paradox') — a TIMESCALE constraint, not an outcome one

plasticity_rules.name: Three-factor neuromodulatory gating (DANGER POINT)
plasticity_rules.does: lets reward/novelty/arousal sculpt which coincidences survive
plasticity_rules.constraint: THE modulator must be the OUTPUT of a circuit set by its wiring — NEVER a value we set because we judge an outcome good/bad. This is where outcomes are most easily smuggled back in.

plasticity_rules.name: Developmental gating
plasticity_rules.form: all updates scaled by eta(age, circuit)
plasticity_rules.does: high plasticity early, dips, adolescent resurgence, low in adulthood
plasticity_rules.constraint: age enters ONLY here, as a RATE, never as a target; abstracts PV-interneuron/perineuronal-net critical-period machinery

plasticity_rules.name: Structural plasticity
plasticity_rules.form: prune weights held ~0 too long; add connections between co-active adjacent circuits within connectome limits
plasticity_rules.does: activity-dependent wiring change; overproduction-then-pruning; the 'growth from stimulation'
plasticity_rules.constraint: additions bounded by anatomical adjacency/connectome; not arbitrary

plasticity_rules.name: Bounds & competitive normalisation
plasticity_rules.form: weights within finite bounds; incoming weights normalised
plasticity_rules.does: makes learning competitive — strengthening some costs others
plasticity_rules.constraint: finite synaptic resources; competition is a consequence, not a tuned outcome

innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: gustofacial acceptance response present in anencephalic newborns; cross-species and cross-cultural; elicitable with minimal feeding experience; sucrose is analgesic via oral afferents in newborns
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: A hedonic/consummatory response, not a 'food is good' rule; salt is NOT in this class at birth (see PR-SALT)

innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: grimace/gape/tongue-protrusion rejection in anencephalic newborns; cross-species
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: Rejection reflex, not a learned 'poison' concept

innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: aversive facial response in newborns (Rosenstein & Oster)
acceptance-type facial response reported in newborns
hedonic response to salt is equivocal at birth; reports of emergence around ~4-6 months
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: NOT reliably present at birth — an important exception to the 'tastes are innate' generalisation; encode as delayed/uncertain

innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: nociceptive withdrawal and central responses present in preterm/newborn; spinothalamic pathway functional early
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: The birth-present link is nociceptor->aversion/arousal circuitry; it is anatomy, NOT the rule 'pain is bad'. This is the exemplar of the honesty wall.

innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: thermoregulatory drive is innate physiology; neonatal thermal comfort/discomfort responses
C-tactile afferents tuned to gentle stroking; skin-to-skin contact calms newborns and reduces pain responses
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: The affective-touch->calming link is birth-present; the SOCIAL MEANING of contact (who is safe) develops

innate_wiring_catalogue.stimulus: interoceptive homeostatic error (hunger, satiety, thirst, air-hunger/CO2)
innate_wiring_catalogue.target: drive / aversion via arcuate + NTS + insula
innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: homeostatic regulation (feeding, breathing, fluid balance) is innate and functional at birth
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: Drives, not learned goals; the arcuate hunger/satiety machinery is birth-wired

innate_wiring_catalogue.target: subcortical defensive circuit: superior colliculus -> pulvinar -> amygdala (blink/flinch)
innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: unconditioned defensive response across virtually all species incl. human infants; dedicated subcortical pathway
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: Whether the very youngest infants' postural response is defensive vs tracking is debated; timing/accuracy refines with age and mobility

innate_wiring_catalogue.target: acoustic startle reflex: cochlear root -> caudal pontine reticular nucleus -> motor
innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: obligatory brainstem reflex present at birth; short latency; three-synapse arc
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: The raw startle is innate; fear-potentiation of startle (amygdala) is acquired later

innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: newborns preferentially orient to face-like/top-heavy patterns from birth
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: CONTESTED: the innate component may be a NON-specific bias for top-heavy, high-contrast, low-spatial-frequency, temporal-field input rather than a face-specific template; cortical face specialisation (CONLERN) is experience-driven and emerges by ~3 months

innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: newborns preferentially orient to biological-motion point-light displays
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: Specificity and strength less established than looming/startle

innate_wiring_catalogue.stimulus: human voice / infant-directed speech / mother's voice
innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: newborns prefer human voice and their mother's voice
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: IMPORTANT: mother's-voice and native-prosody preferences reflect PRENATAL auditory exposure (learned in utero), so this is partly experience, not purely innate wiring

innate_wiring_catalogue.stimulus: ancestral-threat categories (snakes, spiders, angry/threatening faces, heights)
innate_wiring_catalogue.target: amygdala threat-learning: FASTER acquisition + SLOWER extinction of aversive associations for these categories
innate_wiring_catalogue.present_at_birth: yes (as a bias, not a fear)
innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: aversive conditioning to snakes/spiders is acquired faster and extinguishes slower than to neutral stimuli; monkeys acquire snake (not flower) fear observationally
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: CRUCIAL: this is a bias in LEARNABILITY, not an innate fear. Human INFANTS are NOT afraid of snakes/spiders — they show rapid DETECTION/attention, not fear. Encode as a modifier on the plasticity learning-rate/extinction for these stimulus classes, NEVER as a birth-present fear weight. Evolutionary account is itself contested.
sources: LoBue & Rakison (detection not fear in infants)

innate_wiring_catalogue.stimulus: separation / loss of contact from an attachment figure
innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: separation-distress vocalisations are present at birth across mammals; the PANIC/GRIEF separation system is a birth-present primary-process affect
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: the separation->distress link is birth-present; WHO counts as an attachment figure develops

innate_wiring_catalogue.target: calming/appetitive security substrate (NAc-shell), via IN-SOMATO:affective_touch & IN-INTERO:thermal_warmth
innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: proximity/skin-to-skin contact calms newborns and reduces distress; complements PR-CONTACT (C-tactile) at the social-proximity level
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: proximity->security is birth-present; the identity of the secure base develops

innate_wiring_catalogue.target: separation-distress / social-pain substrate (PAG-PANIC; overlaps aIns/dACC), via IN-INTERO:contact_loss (appr.exclusion)
innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: social exclusion recruits the physical-pain/separation-distress substrate (dACC/anterior insula); the social-pain overlap is early-emerging
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: the rejection->social-pain overlap reuses the separation-distress route; a dedicated social-pain channel is a registered gap, not yet a distinct edge

input_channels.notes: smell; direct limbic access; early mother-odour learning
taste; carries the sweet/bitter/sour primary reinforcers

input_channels.receptors: muscle spindles, Golgi tendon organs, joint receptors
input_channels.central_entry: dorsal column nuclei -> VPL -> S1/parietal; cerebellum

input_channels.receptors: cardiac, pulmonary, gastric, chemo/baro, humoral (glucose, hormones)
input_channels.notes: bodily state; the substrate of FELT emotion via body<->brain loops

physical_endowment.biases: how OTHERS respond to the agent (feeds the future relationship matrix)
physical_endowment.notes: a social-response bias, not a worth judgement; drives asymmetric reactions from other sims

physical_endowment.biases: physical affordances; social dominance signals; some threat/defence outcomes
motor-learning rate ceiling; success of physical action
illness burden; energy; stress load; developmental perturbation
quality of input entering each channel
physical_endowment.notes: low acuity changes what stimuli are even available to learn from
the genetic 'gain' layer; NOT emotion dials — reactivity of wiring, shaped thereafter by plasticity

gaps_register: Default (newborn) weights: literature gives ADULT co-activation patterns, not birth values — most default weights will be flagged assumptions until better-sourced.
Exhaustive projection list: a true connectome-level 'which circuit wires to which' is incomplete; assembly must catalogue coverage and holes.
fMRI/brain-activity correlates of emotional/behavioural states are not yet mapped to circuit dynamics — a target for deeper search (task-fMRI, intracranial, developmental cohorts).
Innate-connection strengths are qualitative (strong/moderate/weak); quantitative birth strengths mostly unavailable.
Interoceptive/bodily feedback coupling gains are unquantified (and must stay mechanism-derived, not outcome-tuned).
Receptor-subtype distributions, neuromodulator spatial gradients, and glial/astrocytic contributions are not represented.
Genotype -> parameter mappings (variant -> reactivity/plasticity gain) are not yet specified.
Developmental-onset ages for many subcortical/affective circuits are approximate and often animal-informed.
Prepared-learning bias magnitudes (e.g. how much faster ancestral-threat conditioning is) are not quantified and are evolutionarily contested.
Plasticity eta(age) schedule refs (e.g. 'amygdala_high_early', 'pfc_low_early_high_late') are qualitative labels, not yet quantified curves.

Social-cognition (mentalizing) dynamics are scaffold and the connection weights are assumptions; the cortical social circuits have no rodent homolog so cannot be audited against the Allen mouse connectome -- they rest on human imaging/lesion literature (Part 3 S1).

Subcortical/limbic connectivity has NOT yet been audited against the Allen Mouse Brain Connectivity Atlas; a one-off coverage pass is scoped in MASTER Part 3 S3 (existence/direction only; weights stay scaffold).

circuits.name: Lateral amygdala
circuits.function: Convergence zone; principal associative fear-learning site

circuits.name: Basal amygdala
circuits.function: LA->CeA relay; context/regulation interface

circuits.name: Central amygdala
circuits.function: Output hub driving defensive responses
circuits.sources: Ciocchi et al. 2010; Fadok et al. 2017

circuits.name: Intercalated cell masses
circuits.function: Inhibitory gate on CeA; substrate of extinction
circuits.sources: Pare et al. 2004; Likhtik et al. 2008

circuits.name: Bed nucleus of the stria terminalis
circuits.function: Sustained/uncertain threat (anxiety)

circuits.name: Periaqueductal grey
circuits.function: Defensive output: freeze (vlPAG) / flight (dPAG)

circuits.name: Dorsomedial/medial hypothalamus
circuits.function: Autonomic/endocrine defensive drive

circuits.name: Locus coeruleus
circuits.function: Noradrenaline arousal broadcast; the plasticity gate (rule R5)

circuits.name: Ventral hippocampus
circuits.function: Context: where/when safe vs dangerous

circuits.name: Ventromedial PFC (infralimbic-type)
circuits.function: Extinction, safety signalling, top-down damping

circuits.name: Dorsomedial PFC (prelimbic-type)/dACC
circuits.function: Sustained-threat attention; fear expression

circuits.name: Superior colliculus -> pulvinar
circuits.function: Fast subcortical visual threat channel (looming)
circuits.sources: Wei et al. 2015; Shang et al. 2015

circuits.name: Caudal pontine reticular nucleus
circuits.function: Acoustic startle arc

circuits.name: Ventral tegmental area
circuits.function: Reward-prediction-error teaching signal; the approach plasticity gate (rule R5)

circuits.name: Substantia nigra pars compacta
circuits.function: Nigrostriatal dopamine to dorsal striatum (vigour, habit)

circuits.name: Nucleus accumbens core
circuits.function: Instrumental approach; motivational vigour

circuits.name: Nucleus accumbens shell
circuits.function: Incentive salience ('wanting') + hedonic impact ('liking') hotspots

circuits.name: Ventral pallidum
circuits.function: Limbic reward output; final common hedonic path

circuits.name: Lateral hypothalamus
circuits.function: Appetitive drive hub; links homeostatic/energy state to VTA (SEEKING)

circuits.name: Orbitofrontal cortex
circuits.function: Outcome/stimulus value; reversal learning; flexible valuation

circuits.name: Dorsal striatum
circuits.function: Stimulus-response habit; automatisation of approach

circuits.name: Lateral habenula
circuits.function: Negative reward-prediction (disappointment); suppresses dopamine

circuits.name: Rostromedial tegmental nucleus
circuits.function: Relays lateral-habenula inhibition onto VTA dopamine (negative RPE)

circuits.name: Pedunculopontine tegmental nucleus
circuits.function: Excitatory/cue drive to VTA dopamine bursts

circuits.name: Paraventricular thalamus
circuits.function: Arousal/salience gating of reward-seeking

circuits.name: Retina
circuits.function: Visual transduction and early spatial/contrast coding

circuits.name: Lateral geniculate nucleus
circuits.function: Thalamic relay to V1; contrast/segregation

circuits.name: Primary visual cortex
circuits.function: Early cortical vision; orientation/edges; ocular-dominance critical period

circuits.name: Ventral visual stream
circuits.function: Object/face/word recognition ('what'); category areas specialise with experience

circuits.name: Dorsal visual stream
circuits.function: Motion/space/action ('where'/'how'); 'dorsal-stream vulnerability'

circuits.name: Cochlea
circuits.function: Auditory transduction; tonotopic decomposition

circuits.name: Auditory brainstem
circuits.function: Ascending auditory relay; interaural sound localisation
circuits.nodes: cochlear nuclei -> superior olive -> inferior colliculus

circuits.name: Medial geniculate nucleus
circuits.function: Thalamic auditory relay to A1

circuits.name: Auditory cortex
circuits.function: Auditory cortical processing; native-phoneme/voice tuning narrows in first year

circuits.name: Somatosensory ascending relay
circuits.function: Ascending somatosensory relay

circuits.name: Ventral posterolateral nucleus
circuits.function: Somatosensory thalamic relay to S1

circuits.name: Primary somatosensory cortex
circuits.function: Somatotopic touch/proprioception

circuits.name: Secondary somatosensory / posterior parietal
circuits.function: Somatosensory integration; body schema

circuits.name: Olfactory bulb
circuits.function: Olfactory relay (BYPASSES thalamus)

circuits.name: Piriform (olfactory) cortex
circuits.function: Olfactory identity; direct amygdala/entorhinal access; early odour learning

circuits.name: Nucleus tractus solitarius (gustatory)
circuits.function: First central gustatory relay

circuits.name: Gustatory cortex
circuits.function: Taste identity/quality
circuits.nodes: anterior insula / frontal operculum (via VPMpc thalamus)

circuits.name: Vestibular nuclei
circuits.function: Balance/head-motion; to thalamus, cerebellum, spinal cord

circuits.name: Dorsolateral prefrontal cortex
circuits.function: Working-memory maintenance/manipulation; top-down goal control; planning

circuits.name: Ventrolateral prefrontal cortex
circuits.function: Response inhibition (stopping); controlled retrieval/selection

circuits.name: Dorsal anterior cingulate cortex
circuits.function: Conflict/error monitoring; effort; expected value of control
circuits.sources: Botvinick et al. 2001; Shenhav et al. 2013

circuits.name: Frontopolar cortex
circuits.function: Prospective memory; branching/multitasking; relational integration; metacognition; the LATEST-maturing cortex
circuits.sources: Burgess et al. 2007; Mansouri et al. 2017

circuits.name: Pre-supplementary motor area
circuits.function: Action selection/sequencing; motor inhibition interface (to STN)

circuits.name: Frontal eye fields
circuits.function: Oculomotor/spatial attention control (top-down)

circuits.name: Mediodorsal thalamus
circuits.function: PFC thalamic partner; sustains working memory via thalamo-cortical loops

circuits.name: Subthalamic nucleus
circuits.function: Hyperdirect 'stop' node; global brake on basal-ganglia output

circuits.name: Associative (dorsomedial) caudate
circuits.function: Frontostriatal executive loop; working-memory input gating
circuits.sources: Frank et al. 2001; Hazy et al. 2006

circuits.name: Basal forebrain cholinergic system
circuits.function: Cortical activation; attentional gain; gates cortical plasticity
circuits.nodes: nucleus basalis of Meynert / medial septum

circuits.name: Hypothalamic oxytocin/vasopressin neurons
circuits.function: Source of oxytocin/vasopressin; social salience and bonding modulation (broadcast like a neuromodulator)
circuits.sources: Ross & Young 2009 (animal); human intranasal-OT effects WEAK/inconsistent

circuits.name: Medial preoptic area
circuits.function: CARE / parental-nurturance hub (function is hormonally gated at parturition/parenting)

circuits.name: Medial amygdala
circuits.function: Social/chemosensory processing; social recognition; part of the 'social behaviour network'

circuits.name: Lateral/ventral septum
circuits.function: Social behaviour; modulation of separation-distress and aggression

circuits.name: Periaqueductal separation-distress column
circuits.function: Separation-distress ('PANIC/GRIEF'); the infant-cry/protest output; soothed by contact/opioids/OT
circuits.nodes: periventricular/periaqueductal separation-distress & vocalisation system (distinct column from the threat PAG)

circuits.name: Ventromedial hypothalamus
circuits.function: Reproductive/sexual and social behaviour (gonadal-steroid gated at puberty); also a defensive node

circuits.name: Nucleus tractus solitarius (visceral)
circuits.function: First central relay of visceral/interoceptive afference (cardiac, pulmonary, baro-/chemo-receptor, gut, vagal)
circuits.nodes: rostral+caudal NTS (general visceral division; the gustatory subdivision is NTS-gust in the sensory system)

circuits.name: Parabrachial nucleus
circuits.function: Relays NTS + lamina-I spinothalamic (pain/thermal/visceral) signals to thalamus, hypothalamus and amygdala

circuits.name: Interoceptive thalamus
circuits.function: Thalamic relay of homeostatic afferents to posterior insula (Craig's encephalised interoceptive pathway)

circuits.name: Posterior insula
circuits.function: PRIMARY interoceptive cortex: representation of the body's physiological state (pain, temperature, itch, visceral, hunger, thirst, air-hunger)

circuits.name: Mid insula
circuits.function: Interoceptive re-representation / integration (posterior->anterior stream)

circuits.name: Anterior insula
circuits.function: Meta-representation / subjective feeling ('material me'), emotional awareness; translates interoceptive states into autonomic responses and closes the corticofugal interoceptive-autonomic loop; interoceptive prediction (allostasis)
circuits.nodes: anterior insula (incl. von Economo neuron area)

circuits.name: Paraventricular hypothalamus (autonomic/neuroendocrine)
circuits.function: Principal integrator of stress/autonomic signals; projects to IML, DMV, NTS, parabrachial and to the median eminence (HPA)
circuits.nodes: PVN parvocellular CRH + pre-autonomic neurons (the magnocellular OT/AVP population is PVN-OT in the affiliation system)

circuits.name: Rostral ventrolateral medulla
circuits.function: Primary sympathetic premotor hub: blood pressure, heart rate, vasoconstriction

circuits.name: Intermediolateral cell column
circuits.function: Sympathetic preganglionic output

circuits.name: Sympathetic ganglia + adrenal medulla
circuits.function: Sympathetic effector ('fight-flight'): cardiac, vascular, metabolic, adrenal

circuits.name: Dorsal motor nucleus of vagus
circuits.function: Parasympathetic visceral outflow (gut, secretion; tonic)
circuits.nodes: dorsal motor nucleus of the vagus (DMX)

circuits.name: Nucleus ambiguus
circuits.function: Parasympathetic CARDIAC outflow (rapid vagal brake on heart rate)

circuits.name: Anterior pituitary (corticotrophs)
circuits.function: HPA relay: CRH -> ACTH

circuits.name: Adrenal cortex
circuits.function: HPA effector: ACTH -> cortisol; glucocorticoid negative feedback to hippocampus/PFC/PVN

circuits.name: Right supramarginal gyrus / temporoparietal junction
circuits.function: Self-other distinction; overcomes emotional egocentricity bias; mental-state attribution
circuits.nodes: right supramarginal gyrus (dorsal-anterior TPJ); posterior TPJ
circuits.sources: Silani et al. 2013 (J Neurosci 33:15466, TMS-causal); Saxe & Kanwisher 2003; Decety & Lamm 2007; Van Overwalle 2009; Schurz et al. 2014

circuits.name: Posterior superior temporal sulcus
circuits.function: Social perception: biological motion, gaze/intention from action, recognising social interactions; feeds mental-state inference
circuits.sources: Allison, Puce & McCarthy 2000 (TiCS); Deen et al. 2015; Isik et al. 2017; Schurz et al. 2014

circuits.name: Precuneus / posterior cingulate cortex
circuits.function: Self-referential processing and perspective-taking; integrative hub of the mentalizing/default-mode network
circuits.sources: Cavanna & Trimble 2006 (Brain); Van Overwalle 2009; Schurz et al. 2014

circuits.name: Anterior temporal lobe / temporal poles
circuits.function: Social-semantic knowledge: person knowledge, social scripts and abstract social concepts drawn on by mentalizing
circuits.sources: Olson, Plaut & Ranganath 2007; Zahn et al. 2007 (PNAS); Frith & Frith 2006; Mar 2011

circuits.name: Ventrolateral ventromedial hypothalamus (hypothalamic attack area)
circuits.function: Hypothalamic attack area: integrates provocation/social-threat to threshold reactive (affective) aggression; a necessary-and-sufficient locus for attack, anatomically distinct from the reproductive VMHdm the seed's VMH represents
circuits.nodes: ventrolateral subdivision of the ventromedial hypothalamic nucleus (VMHvl; Esr1+/SF1)
circuits.sources: Lin et al. 2011 Nature; Falkner & Lin 2014; Hashikawa et al. 2017; Wang et al. 2015 (VMHvl->PAG); distinct from reproductive VMHdm

PB-FACE (catalogue, cited); innate orienting bias -- MAY be a NON-specific top-heavy/high-contrast bias, not face-specific; cortical FFA specialisation (V-ventral) is EXPERIENCE-built, not innate
PB-BIOMOTION (catalogue, cited); innate orienting bias to self-propelled/biological motion
PB-STARTLE (catalogue, cited); sensory limb of the acoustic startle arc (StN->CeA is the downstream link)
PB-VOICE (catalogue, cited); voice/IDS preference -- PARTLY prenatal learning (mother's voice learned in utero), so not purely innate
DISCRIMINATIVE pain (location/intensity) via spinothalamic -- SENSORY, NOT value; the aversive VALUE link is nociception->amygdala in the threat system
direct limbic access; odour value largely LEARNED (associative), so not an innate-value link
PR-BITTER (catalogue, cited); innate aversion/rejection (brainstem gape reflex is a separate innate motor pattern, to be placed with motor)

auditory cue -> amygdala; associative; strength assumed

cognitive control of approach/impulse; weak early; strength assumed

mesocortical DA; D1 inverted-U WM modulation; anatomy
NA (alpha2A) WM/signal-to-noise; stress-level NA impairs PFC; anatomy

cholinergic gain/plasticity in sensory cortex (ACh tunes sensory too); anatomy
cholinergic gain in somatosensory cortex; anatomy

OT DAMPENS amygdala threat (anxiolytic) -> affiliation buffers stress; animal-dominant, contested in humans
parental motivation drives reward/approach toward infant (parturition/parenting-gated); animal-dominant

INNATE separation-distress seed: loss of caregiver contact/warmth drives distress/vocalisation. The infant cry/protest is unlearned and cross-species (Panksepp 1998). Framed as innate WIRING (contact-loss -> distress output), NOT a 'separation is bad' rule. Node-level detail animal-dominant.
OT/opioids LOWER separation distress (reunion/contact soothes); animal-dominant
'social pain': ACC drives distress on social loss (overlaps physical-pain ACC); contested interpretation; strength assumed
social odours -> medial amygdala (e.g. mother-odour learning); main olfactory (human vomeronasal organ is vestigial)
face/social visual cue -> medial amygdala; associative; strength assumed
social/chemosensory -> sexual behaviour (VMH); gonadal-steroid gated at puberty; animal-dominant
sexual-behaviour motor output via PAG; animal-dominant
Craig's thalamocortical interoceptive projection to posterior insula; anatomy
limbic sensory (insula) -> limbic motor (cingulate): feeling -> affective motivation; anatomy
interoceptive contribution to affect/threat (the felt-body component of emotion); strength assumed
emotional evaluation of homeostatic state; anatomy
DESCENDING interoceptive/allostatic prediction to brainstem (von Economo corticofugal loop); PREDICTIVE-processing framing -- emerging
anterior insula translates interoceptive states into autonomic/allostatic control; emerging
limbic drive of autonomic/HPA (CeA -> PVN/NTS); anatomy
dorsomedial hypothalamus modulates ANS/HPA via PVN; anatomy
infralimbic/mPFC cortical regulation of autonomic + HPA; matures late; strength assumed
preganglionic ACh -> ganglia + adrenal medulla; anatomy
glucocorticoid NEGATIVE feedback via hippocampal GR; anatomy
glucocorticoid negative feedback to PVN (self-limiting); anatomy

INNATE INTERIOR alarm: the amygdala chemosenses CO2/acidosis via ASIC1a -> unconditioned fear/panic + sympathetic fight-flight (Ziemann et al. 2009; Klein's false-suffocation-alarm; human ACCN2/panic association). References the catalogue air-hunger term under PR-HOMEOSTATIC. (Medullary serotonergic/RTN chemoreceptors mediate the separate reflexive breathing response.)
